reviewed_map.rationale: Develops open-source base LLMs and provides private deployment and fine-tuning capabilities, forming the core infrastructure layer for AI startups.
Offers a full-stack AI native cloud platform focused on production inference, cost optimization, and model stewardship, enabling efficient deployment of LLMs at scale.
Provides developer tools for debugging, testing, deployment, multi-modal data pipelines, voice assistants, and legal assistance, supporting the construction of agentic AI applications.
citations: YC Blog — The Yc Ai Student Starter Pack

retrieved_docs.title: YC W25 Request for Startups: AI Infrastructure
retrieved_docs.snippet:
YC W25 Request for Startups: AI Infrastructure

Y Combinator's Winter 2025 Request for Startups highlights AI infrastructure as a top investment thesis. Key areas include: developer tools for agentic AI (debugging, testing, deployment), evaluation frameworks for LLM reliability, multi-modal data pipelines, and cost-optimization layers for large language model inference. YC notes that startups solving agent observability and grounding are especially well-positioned.

retrieved_docs.title: YC Blog — The Yc Ai Student Starter Pack
retrieved_docs.snippet:
YC Blog — The Yc Ai Student Starter Pack

This year, YC has partnered with nearly two dozen companies to offer students who attend YC events a suite of free credits for AI devtools.
The YC AI Stack includes over $20,000 in cloud credits on Azure and AWS, over $5,000 in credits for GPT/Claude/Grok, and generous credits for AI devtools in voice, browser automation, web search, video generation, databases, web crawling, and more. We’re especially happy that 14 YC companies are represented in this starter pack.
We would love to see students use these to experiment with the latest AI tools without worrying about paying for them out of pocket. Many of the best ideas are built on top of these technologies. For example, you could build a market research tool using Firecrawl and Exa, a voice assistant using Vapi and Browser Use, or a legal aide using Reducto and Gumloop.
At the moment, students who attend a YC university event starting in Fall 2025 are eligible to redeem a deal. You will receive an email shortly after your eligible event confirming your access to the program. We’re excited to work with our partners to continue rolling this out to more students in the upcoming year. Looking forward to seeing what you all build with

retrieved_docs.title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty

Sponsored
Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
Companies are taking control of their own data to tailor AI for their needs. The challenge lies in balancing ownership with the safe, trusted flow of high‑quality data needed to power reliable insights. This conversation from MIT Technology Review's EmTech AI conference examines how AI factories unlock new levels of scale, sustainability, and governance—positioning data control as a strategic imperative for governments and enterprises.
About the speakers
Chris Davidson, Vice President, HPC & AI Customer Solutions, HPE
Chris Davidson is Vice President of HPC & AI Customer Solutions at Hewlett Packard Enterprise. He leads HPE’s global strategy for AI Factory solutions and Sovereign AI, working with governments, enterprises, and research institutions to build secure, scalable national- and enterprise-grade AI capabilities.
He also directs Product Management and Performance Engineering across HPE’s HPC and AI portfolio, including large-model training platforms and Cray exascale systems. His teams define product strategy, performance architecture, and deployment models that position HPE at the forefront of high-performance and AI c

retrieved_docs.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era

Sponsored
Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
Presented byGC Cybersecurity
Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore. This session from MIT Technology Review's EmTech AI conference explores why security must be rethought with AI at its core, not layered on after the fact.
About the speaker
Tarique Mustafa, Cofounder, CEO, and CTO, GC Cybersecurity
Tarique Mustafa is Cofounder and CEO/CTO of two AI-powered cybersecurity companies: GCCybersecurity, Inc. and its data compliance spinout, Chorology, Inc. A prolific inventor and internationally recognized authority in knowledge representation, inference calculus, and AI planning, Tarique has spent his career applying autonomously collaborative AI to solve complex, ultra-high-scale challenges across cybersecurity, data security, and compliance — with deep expertise spanning Data Classification, DLP, and DSPM industries. His groundbreaking innovations and multiple USPTO patents have earned him global recognition, including frequent invitations to deliver keynote addresses at prestigious internati

retrieved_docs.title: Latent Space: The Rise of Agentic AI Platforms in 2025
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Latent Space: The Rise of Agentic AI Platforms in 2025

The Latent Space newsletter surveys the agentic AI platform landscape as of mid-2025. The analysis identifies three dominant approaches: (1) orchestration frameworks (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph) focused on multi-agent coordination; (2) RAG-first platforms (LlamaIndex, Vertex AI Search) focused on grounding agents in enterprise data; and (3) evaluation-native platforms (Langfuse, Braintrust, PromptLayer) focused on reliability and measurement. The newsletter argues that evaluation will become the key differentiator as models commoditize.

retrieved_docs.title: Together AI Blog — introducing-together-ai-new-look
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Together AI Blog — introducing-together-ai-new-look

If you’ve visited our website or explored our products recently, you might notice something new today. We’ve introduced a refreshed look for Together AI — one that better reflects our mission and approach. Today, we’re excited to share the story behind this evolution.
Our mission
Together AI was founded on the promise of open innovation — helping AI‑native builders create groundbreaking products and experiences across every domain. From day one, we’ve believed that progress happens faster when the tools to build are open and when AI research can quickly be converted into products to deliver faster and better end-user experiences.
In an ecosystem often divided between closed systems and open experimentation, we intentionally chose the open path. Many frontier AI application builders want customization and control — so they can more effectively control the experience for their users as well as manage unit economics as their apps see massive adoption. Additionally, with the field moving so swiftly and new models and technical breakthroughs coming every month, if not every week, these builders need a way to quickly integrate those into their workflows.
Together AI is the AI Native Clou

retrieved_docs.title: Tavily Blog — accelerate-ai-development-databricks-discover-govern-and-build-mcp-and-agent-bricks
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Tavily Blog — accelerate-ai-development-databricks-discover-govern-and-build-mcp-and-agent-bricks

Connect your AI agents to a growing ecosystem of governed external services via the new MCP Catalog and Databricks Marketplace
by Elise Gonzales, Nikhil Gaekwad and Harish Gaur
Data intelligence is transforming how enterprises operate, allowing organizations to build AI agents that reason over vast amounts of proprietary data. A critical component of this evolution is the ability for AI agents to connect to external services and third-party data assets to take real-world actions. This is where the Model Context Protocol (MCP) shines, acting as a standardized way for LLMs to access external tools.
However, leveraging MCP at scale presents three major enterprise challenges:
Databricks now addresses these challenges with a secure, unified, and scalable MCP experience within Agent Bricks: the MCP Catalog (Beta) for discovery and governance, an MCP in Databricks Marketplace (Public Preview) to access a growing ecosystem of external tools, and integration with the Supervisor Agent (Beta) to enable agentic orchestration.
This launch, part of Databricks’ Week of Agents, advances our vision for AI that accurately reasons on your data. By unifying governance, discovery, and real-time action

retrieved_docs.title: Import AI — Import AI 452: Scaling laws for cyberwar; rising tides of AI automation; and a puzzle over gDP forecasting
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Import AI — Import AI 452: Scaling laws for cyberwar; rising tides of AI automation; and a puzzle over gDP forecasting

Import AI 452: Scaling laws for cyberwar; rising tides of AI automation; and a puzzle over gDP forecasting
by Jack Clark
Welcome to Import AI, a newsletter about AI research. Import AI runs on arXiv and feedback from readers. If you’d like to support this, please subscribe.
Uh oh, there’s a scaling war for cyberattacks as well!:
…The smarter the system, the better the ability to cyberattack…
AI safety research organization Lyptus Research has looked at how well AI systems can perform a variety of cyberoffense tasks and found a clear trend of more advanced models being able to do more advanced forms of cyberattack.
“Across frontier models released since 2019, the doubling time is 9.8 months. Restricting to models released since 2024, it steepens to 5.7 months. The most recent frontier models in our study, GPT-5.3 Codex and Opus 4.6, sit above both fitted trendlines, achieving 50% success on tasks taking human experts 3.1h and 3.2h respectively,” they write. “Our most recent open-weight model, GLM-5, lags the closed-source frontier by 5.7 months, suggesting that frontier offensive-cyber capability may diffuse into open-weight form on relatively short timelines.”
